raw_consumer_statement:
-On XX/XX/25 I noticed and reported over {$6300.00} in unauthorized credit card charges on my Citibank card taking place between XX/XX/25 and XX/XX/25. I saw that the address on my account had been fraudulently changed and a new card had been ordered to a state and address where I do not live, and where I had not been ( the charges were all in Florida, I live in New York and had not visited Florida in more than 10 months ). There were 34 fraudulent charges in the vicinity of this new Florida address. 
-I immediately locked the card, reported the fraud online to citi , changed the address back to my correct address, and ordered new cards. 
-On XX/XX/25 I spoke to a citi agent about the fraudulent charges, explained the address change and the charges in a different state, and explained that the card in my possession was not the card that had been fraudulently obtained and used to make these purchases. 
-On XX/XX/25 I received a letter from citi dated XX/XX/25 that I was found responsible for all purchases because the purchases had been made with a chip enabled card reader and the card was in my possession at the time of the transactions. This is not true. 
-I also received a letter from citi dated XX/XX/25 on XX/XX/25 stating that my dispute was under investigation. 
-As of XX/XX/25, the citi website shows for all purchases " Our investigation is complete and a fraud credit will not be issued to your account. We recommend that you review your transaction history to see if the merchant already posted a credit for the transaction before reaching out to us for any questions. '' As stated previously to the citi agent, the card used to make these purchases was NOT in my possession.
